headline: Fuel prices rise on Holy Week | ABS-CBN News
text:
Motorists queue for fuel at a gas station in Pasig City on October 10, 2022, a day before a new round of oil price hike takes effect. Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News 
MANILA (UPDATED)-Fuel prices are rising this Holy Week.
Petroleum companies announced the following price hikes effective March 25, Tuesday:

PILIPINAS SHELL, SEAOIL (effective 6 a.m.)
Gasoline - P2.20/L increase
Diesel - P1.40/L increase
Kerosene - P1.30/L increase

CALTEX (effective 6:01 a.m.)
Gasoline - P2.20/L increase
Diesel - P1.40/L increase
Kerosene - P1.30/L increase

JETTI, PETRO GAZZ (effective 6 a.m.)
Gasoline - P2.20/L increase
Diesel - P1.40/L increase

CLEANFUEL (effective 4:01 p.m.)
Gasoline - P2.20/L increase
Diesel - P1.40/L increase

Holy Week oil price hike ahead: DOE

The Department of Energy said the price hikes are due to several geopolitical developments abroad.
DOE Assistant Director Rodela Romero of the Oil Industry Management Bureau said Ukaine's attacks on three large Russian refineries spooked markets.  
Oil tankers from the Middle East now also have to travel farther to avoid attacks from Houthis in the Red Sea.
“‘Di po ba, iyong ating mga oil vessels, dumadaan sa Red Sea and then nagkakaroon po ng kaguluhan ang Houthis, hinaharang po nila kaya umiikot pa po iyong mga vessels na iyon. Sa pag-ikot po doon, tumataas po iyong freight cost, at the same time po, iyong insurance because of the risk factor,” Romero said.
('Isn't it that our oil vessels, passing through the Red Sea and then the Houthis are having trouble, they are blocking them so those vessels are still going around. By going around there, your freight cost increases, at the same time, your insurance because of the risk factor)
Romero also pointed to the decline in the oil inventory of the United States, and the cut in production from the Organization of Oil Exporting Countries. .
“And lastly po, iyong supply and demand outlook. Sinabi po ng IEA or the International Energy Agency, at saka ng US Energy Information Administration na for 2024, may deficit daw po ng global supply.”
(And lastly, the supply and demand outlook. The IEA or the International Energy Agency, and then the US Energy Information Administration said that for 2024, there is a deficit of global supply.)

headline: 4 suspects remanded in custody over Moscow concert hall massacre | ABS-CBN News
text:
MOSCOW — Four men accused of involvement in a massacre at a Moscow concert hall that killed 137 people were remanded in custody Sunday, as Russia observed a national day of mourning following the attack claimed by the Islamic State.
All four suspects have been charged with terrorism, according to Moscow's Basmanny district court, and face life imprisonment. Their detention is set until May 22 but may be extended depending on the date of their trial.
The court said two of the defendants had pleaded guilty, and one of them, from Tajikistan, had "entirely acknowledged his guilt".

President Vladimir Putin has vowed to punish those behind the "barbaric terrorist attack", and on Saturday said the four gunmen had been arrested while trying to flee to Ukraine. Kyiv has strongly denied any connection to the attack.
Putin has made no public reference to the Islamic State (IS) group's claims of responsibility.
READ: Russia mourns as toll from concert hall massacre climbs to 137
At least 137 people, including three children, were killed Friday evening when gunmen stormed the Crocus City Hall in Moscow's northern suburb of Krasnogorsk then set fire to the building.
It is the deadliest attack in Europe to have been claimed by IS.
Russia's Investigative Committee posted a video of the four suspects being dragged into its headquarters in Moscow.
There was no statement on the other seven suspects arrested in connection with the attack.
Officials have said the gunmen were all foreign nationals.
'Machine guns, knives, firebombs'
The Islamic State group posted Saturday on Telegram that the attack was "carried out by four IS fighters armed with machine guns, a pistol, knives and firebombs" as part of "the raging war" with "countries fighting Islam".
A video lasting about a minute and half, apparently filmed by the gunmen, has been posted on social media accounts typically used by IS, according to the SITE intelligence group.
The video — which appears to have been filmed from the lobby of the concert venue — shows several individuals with blurred faces and garbled voices, firing assault rifles with inert bodies strewn on the floor and a fire starting in the background.
Russian investigators said that after walking through the theatre shooting spectators, the gunmen set fire to the building, trapping many inside.
Health officials said the number of casualties had risen to 182, with 101 people still in hospital, of whom 40 were in "critical" or "extremely critical" condition.
The attack was the deadliest in Russia since the Beslan school siege in 2004.
The emergency situations ministry has so far named 29 of the victims, but the blaze has complicated the process of identification.
The ministry on Sunday posted a video of heavy equipment arriving at the venue to dismantle damaged structures and clear debris.
READ: Pope sends prayers to Moscow victims of 'cowardly' attack
'Morally crushed'
On the streets of the capital on Sunday, there was shock and grief.
"It is a tragedy. I was morally crushed," Ruslana Baranovskaya, 35, told AFP.
"People don't smile... everybody feels the loss," said 73-year-old Valentina Karenina, a pensioner standing on a street off Red Square.
Museums, theatres and cinemas around the country closed and billboards were replaced with memorial posters.
Mourners continued to stream to the concert hall in northwest Moscow to lay flowers as a tribute to the victims.
More than 5,000 people donated blood following the attack, officials said, with many standing in long queues outside clinics.
Abroad, people left floral tributes outside Russian embassies in sympathy.
Putin on Saturday vowed "retribution and oblivion" to the "terrorists, murderers and non-humans" who carried out the "barbaric terrorist attack".
Several of his allies have called for the country to lift a moratorium on the death penalty, sparking concern among Kremlin critics.
 Putin points to Ukraine
Putin's statement Saturday suggested a Ukraine connection.
"They tried to escape and were travelling towards Ukraine, where, according to preliminary data, a window was prepared for them on the Ukrainian side to cross the state border," Putin said of the attackers in his televised address.
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, in his own evening address Saturday, rejected any suggestion that Kyiv had been involved.
In Moscow, some doubted also Putin's claims.
"I'm not inclined to believe the version about Ukraine's involvement... this is more like those committed by Islamist extremists," said Vomik Aliyev, a 22-year-old who often went to the concert hall and who said his parents were Muslim.
Washington also dismissed any suggestion that Kyiv had been involved.
"ISIS (Islamic State group) bears sole responsibility for this attack," said White House National Security Council spokeswoman Adrienne Watson.
Late on Sunday, French Prime Minister Gabriel Attal announced the country was returning to its top security alert status in the wake of the attack and given "the threats weighing on our country".
He attributed the attack to the Islamic State group.
© Agence France-Presse

headline: Hot weather ahead during Holy Week, says PAGASA | ABS-CBN News
text:
Got plans during the Holy Week break? Time to pack an umbrella, Kapamilya, because we've got hot weather ahead in Metro Manila and a large portion of the country starting Wednesday, weather bureau PAGASA said Monday.
PAGASA weather forecaster Veronica Torres said expect possible rains in the southern parts of Luzon and the Visayas, including Bicol region, on Monday and Tuesday but "the rest of the week will be hot in Metro Manila and a large portion of our country."
On Monday, the Easterlies will bring cloudy skies with scattered rainshowers and thunderstorms in Eastern Visayas and Caraga while Metro Manila and the rest of the country will experience partly cloudy to cloudy skies with isolated rainshowers or thunderstorms.
Meanwhile, the dry season in the Philippines is expected to last until the last week of May or the first half of June. "Maiksi lang po ang ating dry season," Torres said. 
PAGASA announced the start of the warm and dry season and the end of the northeast monsoon or amihan season on Friday. 
Dry season is here, says PAGASA
An increase in air temperatures was observed in many parts of the country and the agency said the number of dry and warm days would increase in the coming months. 
A man portraying Jesus Christ is nailed a wooden cross during a reenactment of Christ's suffering and death on Good Friday in Paombong town, Bulacan province, north of Manila, Philippines on April 7, 2023. Basilio H. Sepe, ABS-CBN News

headline: Boracay nearly 100 pct full, says foundation | ABS-CBN News
text:
Heads-up, Kapamilya. Boracay, which was named 2012's Best Island in the World by international travel magazine Travel+Leisure, is getting full ahead of the Holy Week break, a member of the Boracay Foundation board of directors said Monday. 
"Hindi pa 100% puno pero it's getting there. Meron pang makukuhang room," Neneth Graf, Boracay Foundation board member, said in a TeleRadyo Serbisyo interview. 
She said tourist arrivals have reached 6,000-8,000 daily, noting the Department of Environment and Natural Resources has yet to establish rules on the island's carrying capacity.
Graf said tourist restrictions to the island paradise have been eased, with dusk to dawn beach parties already allowed. 
Fire dances are also allowed in front of the Red Coconut Beach Hotel but smoking and drinking on the beach remain disallowed. 
Upcoming events on the island include beach volleyball and dragonboat tournaments. 
Graf reminded the public to transact only with legitimate tour organizers, due to the proliferation of online scammers.

headline: 24-hr road works set in Metro Manila for Holy Week | ABS-CBN News
text:
Members of the Metro Manila Development Authority (MMDA) assist motorists as a carousel bus merges onto private vehicle traffic near the West Avenue area in Quezon City on August 7, 2023. Jonathan Cellona, ABS-CBN News/File
MANILA -- There will be 24-hour road works on major thoroughfares in Metro Manila this Holy Week, according to the Metropolitan Manila Development Authority. 
The MMDA said the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) and a telecommunications firm will take advantage of the summer break, even as a significant number of motorists and travelers head to the provinces for religious observances and family gatherings.
The road works will begin on Holy Wednesday (March 27) at 11 pm and continue round-the-clock until Monday (April 1) at 5 am. 
They will be undertaken at the following areas:
1. Batasan-Commonwealth Tunnel, Filinvest 1 Road to fronting Sandigan (1st lane from plantbox, Quezon City)
2. West Avenue, Ligaya St. to Del Monte Avenue (1st lane from sidewalk), Quezon City
3. Luzon Avenue SB, Congressional Avenue Ext. to before Luzon Flyover (1st lane from center), Quezon City
4. Commonwealth Avenue, Landbank to Elliptical Road (3rd lane from sidewalk), Quezon City
5. Mindanao Avenue, Tandang Sora Avenue to Longines St. (truck lane), Quezon City
6. Mindanao Avenue Tunnel to Sauyo Road (truck lane), Quezon City
7. Payatas Road, Batasan Road to Bayanihan St., Quezon City
8. Payatas Road between Singko St. & Leyte St., Quezon City
9. Payatas Road before Maynilad Pumping Station to Petron Gas Station (1st lane from sidewalk), Quezon City
10. IBP-San Mateo Road, Quezon City
11. Roxas Boulevard NB between Lourdes St. & Vicente Sotto St., Pasay City
12. Roxas Boulevard NB between Remedios St. & Pasaje Del Carmen St., Pasay City
13. Roxas. Boulevard EDSA Flyover SB bridge approach (outer lane & inner lane), Pasay City
14. South Super Highway East Service Road, Parañaque City
15. EDSA NB Guadalupe before bridge (3rd lane), Makati City
16. EDSA NB Guadalupe P. Burgos St. near Jollibee and McDonald's (4th lane), Makati City
17. EDSA NB Guadalupe JP Rizal Ext. (2nd lane), Makati City
18. EDSA NB Guadalupe JP Rizal Ext. near Stoplight (2nd lane), Makati City
Motorists are advised to take alternate routes. The affected roads will be fully passable by 5 a.m. on Monday (April 1).
MMDA Acting Chairman Atty. Don Artes said the Holy Week break is the best time for the contractors to complete their work works and reblocking activities since "fewer vehicles are on Metro Manila streets during that period."
"The clearance for the 24-hour road works aims to strike a balance between maintaining infrastructure and minimizing disruptions and inconvenience to motorists after the significantly religious holidays," he said.

headline: Teves bigo pang mapauwi ng Pilipinas | ABS-CBN News
text: Balik-Pilipinas na si National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Director Medardo de Lemos mula Timor-Leste matapos madakip si dating congressman Arnolfo Teves Jr. Bigo man ang NBI team na mapauwi si Teves sa ngayon, kumpiyansa ang pinuno ng ahensya na maibabalik din nila sa Pilipinas ang pinatalsik na kongresista. Nagpa-Patrol, Niko Baua. TV Patrol, Lunes, 25 Marso 2024

headline: 50 pamilya nawalan ng tirahan sa sunog sa Maynila | ABS-CBN News
text: Nilamon ng apoy ang isang residential area sa Capulong Street, Tondo, Maynila noong gabi ng Linggo. Sa bangketa na muna namamalagi ang ilan sa 50 pamilya na nawalan ng tahanan. Nagpa-Patrol, Karen de Guzman. TV Patrol, Lunes, 25 Marso 2024

headline: Chinese ships mas agresibo sa Ayungin Shoal | ABS-CBN News
text: Mas mapusok at agresibo ang pagmamaniobra ng mga barko ng China para pigilan ang resupply mission sa Ayungin Shoal noong Sabado. Nasira ang isa sa mga barko ng Pilipinas at tatlo ang nasugatan dahil sa lakas ng buga ng tubig na itinutok ng China Coast Guard vessels. Nagpa-Patrol, Jose Carretero. TV Patrol, Lunes, 25 Marso 2024

headline: Pilipinas 'di natitinag sa pangha-harass ng China sa West PH Sea: Teodoro | ABS-CBN News
text: Nagpalitan ng protesta at posisyon ang Maynila at Beijing kaugnay sa muling paggamit ng China ng water cannon sa West Philippine Sea. Hamon ni Defense Secretary Gilbert Teodoro ay humarap ang Tsina sa arbitration court kung tiwala itong may basehan ang kanilang pag-angkin sa halos buong South China Sea. Nagpa-Patrol, Katrina Domingo. TV Patrol, Lunes, 25 Marso 2024

headline: Pagbibigay-impormasyon ukol sa pertussis hinimok | ABS-CBN News
text: Sa pagtaas ng kaso ng pertussis sa National Capital Region, naniniwala si Metro Manila Council Chairman Francis Zamora na bukod sa bakuna, kailangan din ang malawakang pagbibigay-impormasyon partikular sa mga magulang, para mas maiwasang tamaan ng sakit ang kanilang mga anak. Nagpa-Patrol, Raphael Bosano. TV Patrol, Lunes, 25 Marso 2024

headline: Holy Week 2024: Mga biyahero dumarami na sa mga paliparan, pantalan | ABS-CBN News
text: Nagsimula nang dumami ang mga pasahero sa mga paliparan at pantalan ngayong Semana Santa. May paalala ang pamunuan ng airport sa mga pasahero para masegurong komportable at maayos ang kanilang biyahe. Nagpa-Patrol, Jekki Pascual. TV Patrol, Lunes, 25 Marso 2024

headline: 3 patay sa imburnal sa QC | ABS-CBN News
text: Isang tubero at dalawang helper ang patay matapos umanong hindi makahinga nang bumaba sa isang imburnal sa Katipunan Avenue, Quezon City. Nagpa-Patrol, Zyann Ambrosio. TV Patrol, Lunes, 25 Marso 2024

headline: 3 patay matapos ma-suffocate sa imburnal sa Quezon City | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA -- Tatlong lalaki ang namatay matapos mag-inspeksyon sa isang imburnal sa Katipunan Avenue sa Quezon City Biyernes ng gabi.
Sa cellphone video na kuha ng mga rescuers mula sa Barangay Loyola Heights Quezon City, makikitang sinusubukang i-revive ang isa sa tatlong lalaking nawalan ng malay habang nag-iinspeksyon sa isang imburnal sa Katipunan Avenue.
"Nakita namin bukas itong manhole na ito, inaangat na ng tali, inaangat yung victim unconscious. Paglabas pinahiga namin... naka monitor kami sa vital signs. Wala na po kaming makuha. Ang declaration zero-zero pero patuloy po kami nagre-revive," kuwento ng rescuer na si Jess Mirasol.
Ayon sa hepe ng Anonas Police Station, unang bumaba sa imburnal para mag inspeksyon ang 55-taong gulang na tubero. Pero nang paakyat na ito ay biglang siyang nawalan ng malay at nahulog.
Sinubukan siyang i-rescue ng dalawa pang kasamahan pero nawalan rin sila ng malay nang bumaba sa imburnal.
Ayon kay Lt. Col. Ferdinand Casiano, hepe ng Quezon City Police District Station 9, nahulog sa butas ang unang biktima, na tinulungan ng ikalawang biktima. Ngunit nahulog din ito at tinulungan ng ikatlong biktima, na nahulog din sa manhole.
"Unconscious. Lumalabas na ang tatlong ito na biktima ay walang safety equipment o walang protective equipment tulad ng gas mask, oxygen mask, harness," ani Casiano.
Naitakbo ang tatlong biktima sa ospital pero idineklara silang dead on arrival. Base sa autopsy ng tatlo, asphyxia due to chemical gas inhalation ang ikinamatay ng tatlo.
Inaresto ng QCPD ang safety officer na kasama ng tatlong biktima. "Sa kaso na ito dapat may managot, so kinasuhan natin yung safety officer ng 3 counts ng reckless imprudence resulting in homicide, dahil sa tatlong namatay," ani Casiano.
Sinubukan ng ABS-CBN News na kunan ng pahayag ang safety officer pero tumanggi muna nitong magsalita.  Pero kinumpirma niya at ng mga tauhan ng contractor ng water company na magbibigay sila ng danyos sa mga pamilya ng mga biktima.
Paalala ng pulis at ng opisyal ng barangay, importanteng ipaalam sa kanila kung may ganitong  delikadong aktibidad.
"Siguro sa lahat ng mga contractor na dadaan sa Barangay Loyola Heights magpaalam sa amin dahil coordination nandoon sa barangay at contractor, para kung mangyayari hindi lang isa ang nakaka-alam at  magkaka-responde mula sa barangay," ayon kay Barangay Loyola Heights Chairman Darwin Hayes.
"Dapat pa pag tayong construction company ay mayroon rin tayong pananagutan sa ating mga empleyado," ayon pa kay Casiano.
Paaala naman ng mga awtoridad, importanteng magsuot ng safety gear tulad ng gas mask, oxygen tank at  harness para sa kaligtasan, lalo na kung susuong sa mga ganitong gawain.

headline: Court dismisses tax evasion case vs Napoles | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA -- Citing the Prosecution’s failure to prove guilt beyond reasonable doubt, the Court of Tax Appeals (CTA) has junked the P44.7-million tax evasion case against “pork barrel queen” Janet Lim Napoles.
In an 84-page decision promulgated on March 21, the CTA Second Division dismissed the 12 cases lodged versus Napoles who was accused of violating the National Internal Revenue Code (NIRC) of 1997.
“After careful consideration of the evidence presented by the plaintiff and the accused, the Court finds that the plaintiff failed to discharge the burden to prove the elements of the crimes charged against accused with proof beyond reasonable doubt,” the Court said.
Janet Lim Napoles gets 138-150 years for graft, malversation conviction over PDAF
“This Court, has no basis for the imposition of civil liability upon the accused,” it added.
The decision was penned by Associate Justice Corazon Ferrer-Flores.
The investigation, initiated by the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR), claimed that Napoles and her husband, Jaime Napoles, underdeclared their income from 2004 to 2009, did not file their Income Tax Returns (ITR) from 2010 to 2012, and did not pay the right tax thereafter.
Napoles found guilty, lawmaker acquitted in P20 million PDAF case
According to the Joint Complaint-Affidavit, however, the spouses Napoles,“were able to purchase real properties, a number of motor vehicles, several insurance policies and club shares and that they have also invested tens of millions of pesos in various new corporations, which acquisitions and investments, they could have conceivable acquired given the income they stated in their ITRs.”
“A comparison of the respective incomes declared by Spouses Napoles vis-a-vis their expenses for TYs 2004, 2006, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011 and 2012 clearly indicates that the expenditures exceed their declared income per ITRs,” the affidavit further said.

headline: Marcos, wife recover from 'flu-like symptoms' | ABS-CBN News
text:
President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. PCO handout photo
MANILA -- President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and First Lady First Lady Marie Louise Araneta-Marcos have completely recovered from their flu-like symptoms and are now in excellent health, the Presidential Communications Office announced Monday.
In a statement, the PCO said the first couple’s attending physician has confirmed “that they are free from all symptoms, enabling them to promptly return to their regular duties, effective immediately.”
“The President has been in meetings today and will resume his public duties tomorrow,” the statement read.
The first couple last week were ordered to rest by their doctor after experiencing flu-like symptoms shortly after their official trips to Germany, the Czech Republic, and Australia.
Following foreign trips, Marcos Jr., wife develop flu-like symptoms: PCO
Marcos Jr., First Lady health improving amid flu-like illness

headline: 2 bus drivers test positive for drugs at PITX | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA -- Two bus drivers tested positive for drugs at a random drug test conducted at the Paranaque Integrated Terminal Exchange (PITX) on Monday, ahead of the expected influx of passengers for the long Holy Week break.
According to PITX spokesman Jason Salvador, of the 123 bus drivers who went through a random drug test, 2 tested positive.
Salvador clarified these need a confirmatory test but the LTO and PITX management decided that the drivers would no longer proceed with their scheduled trips to ensure the safety of passengers.
“Nagsagawa pa tayo ng rabdom drug testing…ginagawa natin ito regularly kasama yung pag-check ng roadworthiness ng mga sasakyan upang magkaroon ng peace of mind yun ating mga kababayan na babiyahe, at the same time para matiyak yung kanilang ligtas na paglalakbay,” Salvador told ABS-CBN News.
(We're doing random drug testing, we're doing this regularly, along with testing of road worthiness of vehicles to have peace of mind of our passengers at the same time to ensure safety of travel.)
Department of Transportation (DOTr) Sec. Jaime Bautista led the inspection at the PITX together with Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) Sec. Benhur Abalos, PNP chief Gen. Benjamin Acorda Jr., and other top government officials.
Bautista said he is satisfied with the security measures in place at the terminal.
He also called on the public not to patronize unregistered or "colorum" vehicles.
“Maraming gustong magsamantala, maraming mag-ooperate ng colorum, iwasan nating sumakay sa colorum na sasakyan dahil hindi tayo sigurado kung ito ay road-worthy and sumusunod sa alintutunin ng LTO at LTFRB,” Bautista said.
(A lot of people wanted to take advantage, some will operate colorum, let's avoid riding colorum vehicles since we're not sure if they're road worthy and if they're following LTO and LTFRB rules)
Meanwhile, Acorda said they have not monitored any security threat this season but added that they are on heightened alert.
Department of Information and Communications Technology (DICT) Sec. Ivan John Uy also joined the inspection on Monday and advised the public to take extra precaution especially against scammers.
24 HOUR ROAD WORKS
Meanwhile, Metropolitan Manila Development Authority (MMDA) chairman Romando Artes announced that the government has allowed 24 hours road diggings starting Wednesday until April 1.
According to MMDA, the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) and a telecommunications firm will undertake 24-hour road works on major roads in Metro Manila during Holy Week, while a significant number of motorists and travelers will be heading to provinces for religious observances and family gatherings.
"Starting Wednesday 11 p.m. i-a-allow na po ng MMDA ang lahat  road diggings 24 hours hanggang Monday na po yan…nasa 100 na road diggings sa buong Metro Manila...baka kayo po ay magulat, simultaneous po yan,” Artes said.
(Starting Wednesday 11 p.m., we will allow the MMDA to do 24/7 road diggings until Monday. Around 100 road diggings in the entire Metro Manila. So don't be surprised, that's simultaneous)
PITX management is expecting foot traffic at the terminal to reach 1.7 million from March 22 to April 1, Easter Monday.

headline: Poe blasts China following Ayungin incident | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — A senator on Monday lashed out against China over its aggression against Philippine vessels delivering supplies to BRP Sierra Madre in Ayungin Shoal in the West Philippine Sea.
DFA summons Chinese envoy after latest Ayungin encounter
For Sen. Grace Poe, China’s latest act of water cannon attacks on the Philippines' resupply mission team was an “uncivilized action.”
“We deplore the latest water cannon attack by China on a Philippine supply vessel that injured our three Navy personnel. This uncivilized action should stop.  We must hold the Chinese vessel responsible for the injury inflicted on our troops,” Poe said in a statement.
Sen. Francis Tolentino meanwhile urged President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. to include in his foreign trips’ agenda a proposal of having an “executive agreement” with the host country for a joint research and exploration related to food and natural deposit issues in the West Philippine Sea.
This kind of alliance, he believes, will not result in further tension between the Philippines and China.
Can Manila diffuse tensions in the South China Sea?
 “Sa halip na mag-concentrate lang tayo sa mga defense with our allies, ay pumasok na rin tayo sa mga ibang kasunduan patungkol sa pagre-research sa agham at teknolohiya, na makakadagdag sa ating food security situation, at maging sa ating energy requirements,” said Tolentino, chairman of the Senate Special Committee on Maritime and Admiralty Zones.
An executive agreement, according to Tolentino, will only require the approval of the heads of states and no longer a treaty and a congressional ratification for it.
Philippines accuses China of installing floating barriers in South China Sea

headline: BI warns public vs fake eTravel websites, social media scams | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — The Bureau of Immigration (BI) is warning the public against fake websites that are charging thousands of pesos for the issuance of eTravel passes.
BI deputy spokesperson Melvin Mabulac reminded travelers that registration for eTravel passes is free to the public.
“Kaya nga po we are asking them if ever po magri-register we go to the official website po sa etravel.gov.ph para hindi po kayo maloko. At kung if ever po mayroon po magsumbong po kayo sa ating cybercrime – iyong Cybercrime Investigation Council sa kanilang hotline number po iyong hotline 1326. Iyan po ang babala natin sa ating publiko,” Mabulac said.
(That's why we are asking them to register at the official website. Please report any complaint to our cybercrime with hotline 1326.)
The BI is coordinating with the DICT as well as with law enforcement agencies to take down fake websites and make the people behind them accountable to the law.
He also called on the public to be wary of social media posts offering employment, noting that they may be used by human traffickers.
“Nakikita po natin usually talaga is iyong offer sa kanila, iyong trabaho. We’ve also recorded regarding sa presentation – they are going abroad parang mail-order spouse ‘no. They are being invited to go abroad para mag-join daw or they are travelling with claimed spouse only to find out noong nakita natin iyong dokumento though they are authentic, genuine but the entries, information doon ay hindi naman totoo, walang nangyaring kasal – those are the things na nakikita natin,” he said.
HOLY WEEK MANPOWER
The Bureau of Immigration meanwhile assured that they have enough personnel to address the expected exodus of travelers this Holy Week break.
"Kaya nga po, ang ginawa po ng ating opisina, nagdagdag tayo ng around 65 augmentation na personnel, sila po ay tutulong sa ating mga paliparan to ensure na ang proseso sa immigration ay tuluy-tuloy considering na dumarami po iyong ating travelers ngayon,” Mabulac said.
Mabulac said they have already seen an increase in departing passengers at around 11 percent. 
From their daily average for departures this March at 42,900 passengers, it has now reached 47,500 starting March 22. 
Mabulac said that the BI have already prohibited their personnel from going on leave from March 22 until April 1, especially in the international ports.
“We are also utilizing iyong ating mobile counters, if ever marami-rami iyong tao na pasahero, then we are going to utilize that,” he said.

headline: Health centers to remain open this Holy Week to give pertussis vaccines: DOH | ABS-CBN News
text:
Health workers prepare a diphtheria, tetanus and pertussis vaccines during a general health check up for children in Banda Aceh, Indonesia, August 8, 2023. Hotli Simanjuntak, EPA-EFE/File
MANILA -- The Department of Health is intensifying efforts to administer vaccines against pertussis or whooping cough, as cases in the country continue to rise, an official said Monday.
DOH Undersecretary Eric Tayag said at least 453 cases of pertussis nationwide with 35 deaths, compared to last year’s 23 cases. Quezon City was first to declare an outbreak of the disease, with Iloilo City likely to follow suit.
The DOH has attributed the increase in cases to a decline in immunization among Filipinos, especially during the pandemic.
“Sa ngayon po ay hindi na nadagdag iyong listahan bagama’t marami ng lugar dito sa Metro Manila. Kasi doon sa 453 cases, may 167 na nakumpirma; 38 doon sa National Capital Region; sinusundan iyan ng dalawang rehiyon, iyon po ay Calabarzon at saka sa Central Visayas,” Tayag said in a televised briefing.
(So far there has been no addition to the list although there are many places here in Metro Manila. Because out of 453 cases, 167 were confirmed; 38 in the National Capital Region; followed by two regions, that is Calabarzon and then Central Visayas.)
READ: PH to get around 1M doses of pertussis vaccines in June --DOH
READ: Vaccine available against pertussis, DOH says
“Halos sampung rehiyon ang may pagtaas doon sa kaso ng tuspirina, at dito sa National Capital Region ay nagpa-plateau – ibig sabihin ay hindi tumataas katulad ng inaasahan natin. Subali’t binibilang pa po ang maaaring mai-report sa Department of Health at magkakaroon po tayo ng update tungkol po sa mga pagbibilang po tungkol diyan,” he continued.
(About ten regions had an increase in the case of pertussis, and here in the National Capital Region it plateaued – meaning it did not increase as much as we expected. However, those that can be reported to the Department of Health are still being counted and we will have an update on the number.)
Tayag said health centers across the country will be opened on Holy Week for the implementation of catch-up vaccinations against pertussis and other diseases, including measles.
“At inaasahan namin na kahit ngayong Mahal na Araw ay magbubukas iyong mga health centers; kaunting sakripisyo para nang sa ganoon ay i-consider po nila iyan kasi hinahabol natin iyong oras po sapagka’t talagang nakakahawa iyong tuspirina. At kung mayroon pa kayong mga bakuna na maaaring ibigay ay ibigay na po ninyo. At kami naman sa Kagawaran ng Kalusugan ay hinahabol namin para nang sa ganoon, iyong mga kulang na bakuna po ninyo ay mai-deliver na po namin,” he said. 
(And we expect that even this Holy Week our health centers will be open; a little sacrifice because we are chasing time because your pertussis is really contagious. And if you still have vaccines that can be given, please give them. And we at the Department of Health are trying to catch up to deliver your missing vaccines).
Meanwhile, Tayag said the DOH is hoping that catch-up immunizations will also address rising cases of measles especially in the Bangsamoro region, where at least three provinces have declared a measles outbreak.
“Mayroon tayong datos sa tigdas – tiningnan namin iyong nakalipas na anim na buwan mahigit 2,600 mula noong September at 1,400 plus dito ay naitala ng BARMM kaya nga mayroon silang outbreak ngayon sa tatlong lugar ang Lanao del Sur, Maguindanao del Norte at Sulu,” he said.
(We have data on measles – 2,600 cases have been recoded in the last six months since September and over 1,400 of these was recorded in BARMM so they have an outbreak now in three places: Lanao del Sur, Maguindanao del Norte and Sulu.)
READ: Health expert urges vaccination, face masks amid pertussis outbreak
READ: What is pertussis?
To address the rising cases of measles in BARMM, the DOH will be implementing non-selective immunization, Tayag said.
“Ibig sabihin niyan, regardless kung ikaw ay tumanggap o hindi ng bakuna, babakunahan iyong bata para nang sa ganoon ay mabilisan kasi. Madi-delay kung tatanungin po o hihingiin iyong katibayan sa card kung nabakunahan na o hindi. Kung nag-aalala kayo na baka sumobra, ma-overdose, wala pong ganoong sa bakuna. Kaya iyan ay ipaliliwanag doon sa mga kababayan natin sa BARMM para nang sa ganoon ay maintindihan nila at maalis natin iyong vaccine hesitancy na baka may agam-agam sila, isip nila ay nabakunahan na bakit uulitin o kaya nagka-tigdas na bakit bibigyan,” he said.
(That means, regardless of whether you received the vaccine or not, your child will be vaccinated so the process is quicker. There will be a delay if you will be asked or asked for proof on the card if you have been vaccinated or not. If you are worried that you might overdose, there is nothing like that in the vaccine. So that will be explained to our compatriots at BARMM so that they can understand and we can remove that vaccine hesitancy, as they may have doubts, they may think that they have already been vaccinated so why should they have it again, or if they already had measles why should they be given vaccines again?)

headline: China lodges protest vs PH over Ayungin resupply | ABS-CBN News
text:
A Chinese Coast Guard ship attacks a Philippine resupply ship with water cannon near Ayungin Shoal in the West Philippine Sea. From AFP handout video
MANILA -- The Chinese Embassy in the Philippines has filed a protest over what it claims as the “illegal trespassing of Philippine resupply vessels" near Ayungin Shoal.
This, after the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) on Monday said it has summoned officials of the Chinese Embassy to express the Philippines' "strong protest" over the "aggressive actions" by the China Coast Guard and its maritime militia last Saturday against Philippine vessels in Ayungin Shoal.
Ren’ai Jiao is China’s name for Ayungin Shoal, which is known internationally as Second Thomas Shoal.
In a statement, the Chinese Embassy insisted that the resupply vessels intruded in Chinese territory and attempted to bring in construction materials to BRP Sierra Madre, a move which it called “deliberate and provocative” that allegedly “infringed upon China’s sovereignty, maritime rights and interests.” 
The embassy also claimed that the China Coast Guard responded in a lawful and professional manner, as it remains “committed to resolute measures in safeguarding its territorial sovereignty and maritime rights and interests.” It further insisted that China’s sovreignty in the area is grounded on history and law, and called the 2016 Arbitral award, “illegal, null and void.”
Meanwhile, the Philippines thanked the international community for supporting the country in the latest incident of Chinese aggression in the West Philippine Sea.
The list of countries, which has been expanding since the incident last Saturday, include the US, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, South Korea, EU, France, Italy, Germany, Finland, the Netherlands and Sweden. 
The said countries have opposed China’s use of force during the Philippines’ latest rotation and resupply mission to Ayungin Shoal that resulted yet again, to injury among Filipino crew and damage to a civilian vessel. The countries also reiterated calls to abide with international law and to recognize the 2016 Arbitral Award that recognizes the Philippines’ maritime rights within its Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ).
In a statement, the DFA expressed its gratitude for the outpouring of support from the international community.
“The DFA thanks the international community for their continued expressions of support to the Philippines and the defense of the international order based on international law,” the DFA said.
The DFA has summoned the Chinese Embassy’s Charge d’ Affaires, filed a démarche in Beijing, and expressed its strong protest against China’s latest aggression through a phone call between Foreign Affairs Undersecretary for Bilateral Relations and ASEAN Affairs Ma. Theresa P. Lazaro and Chinese Vice Foreign Minister Chen Xiaodong.
DFA summons Chinese envoy after latest Ayungin encounter
In its latest statement, the DFA emphasized it is committed to utilizing diplomatic channels without sacrificing the country’s rights and interests.
“The Department remains committed to take all appropriate diplomatic actions to protest the violation of the Philippine sovereign rights and jurisdiction and violations of international law, particularly UNCLOS. As mentioned in the NTF- WPS Statement of 23 March 2024, the Philippines will not be deterred by threats or hostility, from exercising its legal rights over its maritime zones, including Ayungin Shoal, which forms part of the Philippine EEZ and continental shelf.”
In an earlier statement, the Philippine DFA stressed that “China has no right to be in Ayungin Shoal, a low-tide elevation that lies within the Philippines’ exclusive economic zone and continental shelf in accordance with the 1982 UNCLOS and as affirmed by the 2016 Arbitral Award.
The US State Department also previously said that China has no legal maritime claims around Ayungin or Second Thomas Shoal.
RELATED VIDEO

headline: Villanueva: Work-from-home law can ease Metro Manila traffic woes | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — Putting Metro Manila under a "state of calamity" will not solve the region's monstrous traffic problem, Sen. Grace Poe said Monday.
Metro Manila traffic a state of calamity: Biz group
What the government should do, she said, is seek the advice and listen to the recommendation of experts on how to improve the situation.
“They don’t need to declare a state of traffic calamity. We feel it every single day. Government should listen and employ the help of experts from all sectors... Clearly this is a crisis that needs to be addressed. Adding more roads is not the only solution,” said Poe, chairperson of the Senate Public Services Committee.
The senator issued their statement following the call of the Management Association of the Philippines (MAP) to declare a state of calamity in Metro Manila over its worsening traffic.
“Mass transit should be improved and expanded. Public Electric Vehicles are already being utilized in many countries. Our air quality can certainly improve if public buses are converted to e-vehicles,” she added.
Senate Majority Leader Joel Villanueva meanwhile stressed the need to observe Republic Act No. 11165 or the Work-from-Home law to ease the country’s traffic situation.
“Companies and employees should be allowed to arrange for flexible work arrangements instead of having employees report physically in the office every day and losing working hours due to traffic,” Villanueva said.  
“It is also important for the government to study the proposal on the gradual conversion of public buses into electric vehicles and how this will help make the lives of Filipino commuters more convenient and lessen the environmental impact of traffic congestion in the country,” he added.
Last year, Villanueva filed Senate Resolution No. 859, which calls for the Department of Transportation (DOTr), Metropolitan Manila Development Authority (MMDA) and the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) to review and revisit the government’s traffic control program.
A previous study estimated that the Philippines loses P3.5 billion economically daily due to the traffic problem in Metro Manila.

headline: Barko ng Indian Coast Guard nakadaong sa Maynila | ABS-CBN News
text:
MAYNILA — Nasa bansa ngayon para sa port call ang Samudra Paheredar, isang barko ng Indian Coast Guard. 
Ang Samudra Paheredar — Ocean Guardian sa Ingles — ang ikalawang pollution control vessel ng Indian Coast Guard.
Ginawa ang barko para kontrolin ang mga oil spill at magamit sa rescue operations ng India.
RELATED: India offering 7 helicopters to PH Coast Guard: envoy
Sabi ni Commo. Algier Ricafrente, deputy chief of coast guard staff for international affairs, layon ng pagbisita na ipakita ang kakayahan ng barko sa pagresponde sa mga polusyon gaya ng oil spill at sa search and rescue.
"Marami tayong naka-lineup na activity kasama sa naka-lineup yung training and demonstration ng kanilang capability on marine environmental protection at magkakaroon din tayo ng maritime law enforcement," ani Ricafrente.
Sa ngayon ay onshore muna ang kanilang mga training.
Magkakaroon din ng coastal cleanup drive sa may Dolomite Beach sa Roxas Boulevard bukas.
Ang pagbisita sa bansa ay wala namang kinalaman sa nangyayaring sigalot sa West Philippine Sea.


"Ito kasing visit nila we are focusing on humanitarian sevices kaya pinadala nila dito is yung kanilang marine environmental protection vessel. We are also conducting joint training doon sa search and rescue kasi yun ang mga kailangan natin."
Magkakaroon ng bilateral meeting sa Indian Coast Guard ngayong taon para ilatag ang mga as part of signing ng MOU natin.
